steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $\frac{18{x}^{2}}{5}+4x-\frac{35}{6}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Multiply the cross terms and constant
steps.content:
$$\left(\frac{3}{5}x\right)(-5)=-3x$$
$$\left(\frac{7}{6}\right)(6x)=7x$$
The $x$-terms combine to $-3x+7x=4x$, and the constant is $$\frac{7}{6}\cdot(-5)=-\frac{35}{6}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: So $$g(x)h(x)=\frac{18}{5}x^2+4x-\frac{35}{6}.$$ That matches the computed answer, so D is correct. The coefficient, middle term, and constant all match D, so no rearrangement of the expression is needed.
